title: CLAUDE.md — landscapeR
text: Orientation Claude Code agents working package.

title: CLAUDE.md — landscapeR
text: landscapeR R package implementing two-stage analysis pipeline multi-layer omic data: Stage 1 — Comparative decomposition (GSVD / HO-GSVD): builds state space whose axes explicitly contrast biological layers (disease vs control, temperature conditions, etc.), automatically separating confounders signal. Stage 2 — Quasi-potential dynamics: coordinates, fits U(x) = −log p(x) (log-density inversion) reads critical points barrier heights — tipping points irreversibility biological state transitions. method generalises Rockne-Frankhouser state-transition work (published Cancer Research 2020, Leukemia 2024) plain SVD comparative GSVD/HO-GSVD, MATLAB scripts principled, testable R package. Three biological instantiations planned: reptile TSD (Pogona), islet/diabetes multi-omic, CML/AML reference disease context.

what: Architecture — read this before touching anything
title: CLAUDE.md — landscapeR
text: design contract-based S4. Nine inviolable rules (decisions/): Program interfaces (VIRTUAL classes), implementations One container flows stages (StateTransitionData) Orchestration (targets) separate computation Stage functions pure — input, output, randomness seeded Validate boundaries — typed failure, never raw exceptions Configuration modification — algorithms chosen PipelineConfig, code Registry, switch statements — register_strategy() / get_strategy() Provenance first-class — every artifact records made Abstract joints actually swap; plain code inside algorithms Violating rules creates multi-year maintenance traps design explicitly names. Don’t convenience.

title: CLAUDE.md — landscapeR
code: R/   00-package.R        imports   01-schema-version.R SCHEMA_VERSION constant ("0.1.0")   02-ground-truth.R   GroundTruth class hierarchy (SubspaceGroundTruth,                       TopologyGroundTruth, PotentialGroundTruth)   03-container.R      StateTransitionData (MAE subclass) + validity + migration   04-stage-result.R   StageResult + stage_success() / stage_failure()   05-config.R         PipelineConfig   06-provenance.R     ProvenanceStep + record_provenance()   07-registry.R       .registry, register_strategy(), get_strategy(), list_strategies()   08-contracts.R      All 7 stage VIRTUAL classes + generics   09-boundary.R       validate_boundary() — runs at every stage entry   10-rng.R            setup_rng() — L'Ecuyer-CMRG for parallel reproducibility   11-runner.R         run_pipeline() — sequential dev runner (not for production)  decisions/   README.md           ADR workflow — read before making any algorithm choice   0000-template.md    Template for new ADRs   0001-*              Stage 1 HO-GSVD implementation (provisional)   0002-*              Stage 2 dynamics estimator (provisional-accepted)   0003-*              Rockne-Frankhouser reference code (accepted)   0004-*              Package identity — eigentime vs companion (accepted)  tests/testthat/   helper.R            empty_std() fixture   test-container.R    StateTransitionData validity and migration   test-registry.R     register/get/list round-trips   test-stage-result.R StageResult construction and validation   test-boundary.R     validate_boundary() three-way behaviour

what: ADR workflow — mandatory for algorithm choices
title: CLAUDE.md — landscapeR
text: Every non-trivial algorithm dependency choice needs ADR decisions/ code written. See decisions/README.md. key discipline: define criteria looking results. pick algorithm exists first one found. Stage 0 synthetic controls evidence oracle — algorithm selection driven recovery benchmarks, software availability.

title: CLAUDE.md — landscapeR
text: ADR 0001 (Stage 1 HO-GSVD): multiblock::hogsvd vs Kempf rank-deficient HO-GSVD. registered; Stage 0 thinness sweep rank-deficiency explicit axis decides. rank-deficiency axis critical — diabetes genotype layer rank-deficient. ADR 0002 (Stage 2 estimator): log-density inversion constrained polynomial smoothing. Provisional-accepted. Fill [tbd] thresholds Stage 0 double-well recovery move fully accepted.

title: CLAUDE.md — landscapeR
text: eigentime (drejom/eigentime, private): existing implementation related decomposition + state-transition ideas. Read implementing Stage 1 Stage 2 — duplicate work. ADR 0004 says landscapeR owns everything eigentime stable, check ’s first. Rockne-Frankhouser reference code: cohmathonc/CML_mRNA_state-transition (public, MATLAB). CML paper; different disease algorithm. Stage 2 approach (CML_Potentials.m, CML_fitStationary.m) landscapeR generalises. Key translation: MATLAB pdepe → R deSolve + ReacTran. Design documents (uploaded session, repo): unifiedstatetransitionmethod.md — method concept note v0.2 designspec.md — design specification v0.1

title: Context Map
text: Shared infrastructure — container, registry, provenance, boundary validation, RNG; plumbing every stage depends Stage 0 — synthetic control ladder; generates known-potential ground truth validating Stages 1 2 Stage 1 — comparative decomposition via GSVD/HO-GSVD; builds state-space Stage 2 — quasi-potential dynamics; fits energy landscape reads critical points barrier heights

title: Context Map
text: Shared → stages: Every stage entry exit passes shared StateTransitionData container calls validate_boundary(). provenance recorded via shared infrastructure. Stage 0 → Stage 1: Stage 0 generates synthetic multi-omic matrices Stage 1 decomposes; recovery benchmark measures faithfully Stage 1 recovers ground-truth structure. Stage 0 → Stage 2: Stage 0 generates known-potential trajectories Stage 2 must recover; [tbd] acceptance thresholds ADR 0002 filled Stage 0 results. Stage 1 → Stage 2: Stage 1 produces state-space coordinates (disease axis) Stage 2 uses input quasi-potential estimation.

title: Context Map
text: ADRs span multiple contexts live decisions/. Context-specific decisions may noted within context file formal ADRs always go decisions/.

title: Ubiquitous Language — landscapeR
text: StateTransitionData container produced consumed every stage boundary; carries provenance forward. Stage 1 run K omic layers using HO-GSVD produces one shared disease axis (V*) K layer-specific coordinate matrices (UᵢΣᵢ). Stage 2 run Stage 1 coordinates fits quasi-potential whose wells correspond stable critical points (disease phenotypes) whose barriers quantify irreversibility. double-well potential exactly one unstable critical point (point return); three-well potential two. Every strategy must registered registry selected PipelineConfig — never conditional dispatcher stage code. Stage 0 ground truth honest oracle [tbd] thresholds Stage 2; Stage 2 implementation complete thresholds filled recovery benchmarks. pseudobulk aggregates micro-states macro-state; macro-state encodes state-transition invisible micro-state level. cell-type pseudobulk trajectory spans subset total pseudobulk state-space coordinate range; union ctPsB spans recovers full disease axis.

title: Ubiquitous Language — landscapeR
text: Dev: “Stage 1, run SVD omic layer separately align state-spaces?” Domain expert: “— ’s sequential approach Frankhouser2026 paper: separate SVD per cell-type pseudobulk, project total pseudobulk state-space fact. landscapeR approach HO-GSVD across omic layers , giving shared disease axis jointly optimised. sequential approach aligns post-hoc; HO-GSVD gives alignment algebraically.” Dev: “shared V* HO-GSVD disease axis layers?” Domain expert: “’s set axes common across layers. One disease axis — one whose coordinate separates healthy disease state variables — Stage 2 fits quasi-potential along coordinate. others capture shared confounders want inspect model potential.” Dev: “Stage 0 tells us whether quasi-potential Stage 2 recovers accurate?” Domain expert: “Exactly. Stage 0 generates double-well three-well synthetic dataset known quasi-potential, runs full Stage 1 + Stage 2 pipeline , measures well recovery benchmark recovers known critical points barrier heights. results fill [tbd] thresholds ADR 0002. ’s done, Stage 2 honest calibration.” Dev: “’s rank-deficiency axis thinness sweep?” Domain expert: “diabetes instantiation genotype omic layer rank-deficient — matrix doesn’t full column rank. thinness sweep must include explicit experimental axis verify HO-GSVD handles correctly, just thin-sample cases layers full rank.”

title: Ubiquitous Language — landscapeR
text: “state” overloaded: used biological disease state (Early state, Late state, CML state) mathematical state variable (transcriptome position state-space). code issues: prefer disease state biological phenotype state variable mathematical object. “State-space” “state-transition” compound terms fixed meaning — shorten just “state.” “stage” collides pipeline stage (Stage 0, Stage 1, Stage 2) disease stage (Early state Es, Transition state Ts, Late state Ls). landscapeR contexts always use Stage N (capital S, numeral) pipeline stages disease state biological stages. “potential” used loosely (“potential landscape”) precisely (quasi-potential, specific mathematical object). Always write quasi-potential referring U(x) = −log p(x); reserve “potential landscape” informal explanations . “layer” appears omic layer (mRNA, miRNA), computational layer, occasionally Docker/infrastructure sense (omhq discussions). landscapeR, use omic layer unambiguously; never abbreviate just “layer.” “eigengene” loading value (scalar per gene V*), gene conventional sense. identify gene; quantifies gene’s directional contribution disease axis. Clarify talking biologists may expect name specific transcript. “recovery” used quasi-potential recovery (Stage 2 accurately reconstructs known potential Stage 0 data) loosely biological recovery (treated mouse returning toward healthy state). Always qualify: potential recovery disease recovery.

title: Development log
text: synthetic_potential_control() draws samples known double-well U(x) = (x² − 1)² via Langevin simulation. Provides ground truth Stage 2 validation: wells ±1, barrier 0, barrier height = 1.  Recovery benchmark:
code: # synthetic_potential_control returns a 1-layer object (Langevin coordinates). # Duplicate the layer so hogsvd_averaged (requires >= 2 layers) can run. std_dw  <- synthetic_potential_control(n = 120L, seed = 42L) se_dw   <- experiments(std_dw)[[1L]] std_dw2l <- StateTransitionData(   experiments = list(coords_a = se_dw, coords_b = se_dw),   colData     = colData(std_dw) )  ctor    <- get_strategy("Decomposer", "hogsvd_averaged") std_dw1 <- suppressWarnings(decompose(ctor(), std_dw2l))@value  dctor   <- get_strategy("DynamicsEstimator", "kde_logdensity") std_dw2 <- estimate_dynamics(dctor(), std_dw1)@value plot_potential(std_dw2) # benchmark uses the original single-layer ground-truth object directly bm <- potential_recovery_benchmark(std_dw) knitr::kable(data.frame(   metric = names(bm),   value  = round(unlist(bm), 3) ), col.names = c("Metric", "Value"))

title: Development log
text: 2018 cohort paper’s primary experiment: clean randomised batches, 101 samples (T0–T6 + leukemic endpoint L). 2016 cohort batch-confounded treated supplementary stress-test, projected 2018 state-space. Open question: Gallery shows PC1 highest BC (0.87) η² (0.18) 2018 standalone SVD. paper reports PC2 AML axis PC1 age. Verifying paper figures — section updated confirmed.
code: aml  <- readRDS("../data-raw/aml_mrna_std.rds")  # Run Stage 1 on the primary 2018 layer ctor  <- get_strategy("Decomposer", "hogsvd_averaged") std1  <- decompose(ctor(), aml)@value  # Gallery sorted by bimodality coefficient # PC2 expected to be the AML state-transition axis (PC1 = age in paper) plot_components(std1, colour_by = "condition")

title: Development log
text: Stage 2 extracts quasi-potential Stage 1 coordinates. target: recover double-well landscape reported Rockne2020, CTL samples one well CM/leukemic samples . Current status: Stage 2 implemented runs. -fitting (many wells/barriers) occurs run wrong component (age axis, PC1). Running PC2 pending confirmation PC identity paper. minimum barrier height filter planned next sprint.
code: dctor <- get_strategy("DynamicsEstimator", "kde_logdensity") std2  <- estimate_dynamics(dctor(list(component = 2L)), std1)@value plot_potential(std2)

title: Development log
text: package uses contract/registry pattern — stage algorithms S4 virtual classes generics (decompose, estimate_dynamics). Concrete strategies registered name retrieved runtime: means adding new algorithm (e.g. Kempf HO-GSVD, neural SDE) register_strategy() call changes calling code tests. Last updated: 2026-06-29

title: Authors and Citation
text: Last F (2026). landscapeR: Comparative Decomposition State-Transition Dynamics. R package version 0.1.0.
code: @Manual{,   title = {landscapeR: Comparative Decomposition and State-Transition Dynamics},   author = {First Last},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0}, }

title: landscapeR
text: R package multi-layer omic state-transition analysis. Combines comparative decomposition (GSVD / HO-GSVD) quasi-potential dynamics identify tipping points irreversibility biological state transitions — generalising Frankhouser/Rockne AML work arbitrary multi-omic contexts.

title: landscapeR
text: Stage 1 — Comparative decomposition: builds shared state space across omic layers whose axes explicitly contrast conditions (disease vs control, temperature, genotype). Batch effects confounders separated biological signal design. Stage 2 — Quasi-potential dynamics: fits U(x) = −log p(x) coordinates reads critical points (wells, barriers) barrier heights — tipping points irreversibility state transitions. gallery diagnostic (plot_components()) identifies component carrying state-transition signal without requiring prior knowledge biology.

title: landscapeR
text: Bioconductor dependencies (MultiAssayExperiment, S4Vectors) resolved automatically via pak.

title: landscapeR
code: library(landscapeR)  # Synthetic double-well ground truth std  <- synthetic_potential_control(n = 80L, seed = 42L)  # Stage 1 ctor  <- get_strategy("Decomposer", "hogsvd_averaged") std1  <- decompose(ctor(), std)@value plot_components(std1, colour_by = "group")  # Stage 2 dctor <- get_strategy("DynamicsEstimator", "kde_logdensity") std2  <- estimate_dynamics(dctor(), std1)@value plot_potential(std2)

title: Estimate quasi-potential dynamics on decomposition coordinates — estimate_dynamics
text: Fits \(dX/dt = -\nabla U(X;\,\text{layer}) + \text{noise}\). Critical points = commitment / irreversibility; barrier height = transition difficulty. Cross-sectional (destructive) sampling native data type, workaround.

what: landscapeR: Comparative Decomposition and State-Transition Dynamics — landscapeR-package
title: landscapeR: Comparative Decomposition and State-Transition Dynamics — landscapeR-package
text: S4-based pipeline combining GSVD/HO-GSVD comparative decomposition quasi-potential state-transition modelling multi-layer omic data. Provides contract/registry architecture synthetic validation (Stage 0), archetype classification (Stage 0.5), distributional fit assessment (Stage 0.75), decomposition (Stage 1), dynamics estimation (Stage 2).

title: Gallery plot: all Stage 1 components coloured by a metadata variable — plot_components
text: Use immediately Stage 1 decide component feeds Stage 2.  paper-reported state-transition axis always component 1: Rockne2020 PC2 (age dominates PC1).

title: Gallery plot: all Stage 1 components coloured by a metadata variable — plot_components
code: std <- synthetic_control(n = 40L, p = 500L, K = 2L, signal = 30, seed = 1L) ctor <- get_strategy("Decomposer", "hogsvd_averaged") std2 <- suppressWarnings(decompose(ctor(), std))@value plot_components(std2, colour_by = "group") #> Ignoring unknown labels: #> • colour : "group"

what: Plot singular value spectra with the BBP detectability threshold — plot_spectrum
title: Plot singular value spectra with the BBP detectability threshold — plot_spectrum
text: Shows top singular values omic layer line plot, horizontal reference line Baik-Ben Arous-Péché (BBP) phase-transition threshold \((n \cdot p)^{1/4}\).  Signal components threshold detectable; indistinguishable noise.

what: Measure Stage 2 quasi-potential recovery on a synthetic potential control — potential_recovery_benchmark
title: Measure Stage 2 quasi-potential recovery on a synthetic potential control — potential_recovery_benchmark
text: Runs Stage 2 directly simulated coordinate samples measures accurately recovered well locations barrier height match known ground truth double-well potential \(U(x) = (x^2-1)^2\).

title: Measure Stage 2 quasi-potential recovery on a synthetic potential control — potential_recovery_benchmark
Measure Stage 2 quasi-potential recovery on a synthetic potential control — potential_recovery_benchmark
text: std StateTransitionData synthetic_potential_control() strategy_name character name registered "DynamicsEstimator"

title: Measure Stage 2 quasi-potential recovery on a synthetic potential control — potential_recovery_benchmark
text: named list: well_error (mean absolute error well x-positions), barrier_error (abs error barrier x-position), barrier_height_error (abs error barrier height), n_wells_found, n_barriers_found, elapsed_sec

what: Project a secondary cohort into the state-space of a primary cohort — project_into
title: Project a secondary cohort into the state-space of a primary cohort — project_into
text: Implements Rockne2020 validation projection: $$X_{\rm secondary} \cdot V^*_{\rm primary}$$ places secondary samples coordinate axes primary, without re-fitting decomposition.

title: Project a secondary cohort into the state-space of a primary cohort — project_into
Project a secondary cohort into the state-space of a primary cohort — project_into
text: std_primary StateTransitionData metadata()$stage1 present — supplies \(V^*\) loadings. std_secondary StateTransitionData project.  Must share feature space (genes) primary.  first layer used. layer_primary integer — layer primary object provides \(V^*\) loadings (default 1). layer_secondary integer — layer secondary object project (default 1).

title: Project a secondary cohort into the state-space of a primary cohort — project_into
text: std_secondary metadata()$stage1 written.

title: Project a secondary cohort into the state-space of a primary cohort — project_into

what: Generate one synthetic control dataset — synthetic_control
title: Generate one synthetic control dataset — synthetic_control
text: layer  X_i = signal * outer(u_shared, v_true) + signal_spec * outer(u_spec_i, v_spec_i) + noise. u v vectors unit-norm, signal true singular value shared component.  BBP detectability threshold scale (n*p)^(1/4).

title: Generate one synthetic control dataset — synthetic_control
code: synthetic_control(   n = 40L,   p = 5000L,   K = 2L,   signal = 25,   signal_spec = 8,   noise_sd = 1,   seed = 42L )

title: Generate one synthetic control dataset — synthetic_control
text: n integer number samples (columns omic matrix convention) p integer number features / genes per layer K integer number layers (>= 2) signal numeric singular value shared component (must > 0) signal_spec numeric singular value layer-specific component noise_sd numeric standard deviation additive ..d. Gaussian noise seed integer RNG seed reproducibility

title: Generate one synthetic control dataset — synthetic_control
text: StateTransitionData SubspaceGroundTruth

what: Generate a synthetic potential control via Langevin simulation — synthetic_potential_control
title: Generate a synthetic potential control via Langevin simulation — synthetic_potential_control
text: Simulates samples double-well potential \(U(x) = (x^2 - 1)^2\) using Euler-Maruyama integration overdamped Langevin equation \(dX_t = -U'(X_t) dt + \sqrt{2\beta^{-1}} dW_t\). Samples wrapped StateTransitionData PotentialGroundTruth recording known well locations, barrier position, barrier height.

title: Generate a synthetic potential control via Langevin simulation — synthetic_potential_control
code: synthetic_potential_control(   n = 100L,   beta = 2,   n_steps = 5000L,   dt = 0.01,   seed = 42L )

title: Generate a synthetic potential control via Langevin simulation — synthetic_potential_control
text: n integer number samples collect beta numeric inverse temperature (higher = sharper wells, default 2) n_steps integer Euler-Maruyama steps per sample (default 5000) dt numeric step size (default 0.01) seed integer RNG seed

title: Generate a synthetic potential control via Langevin simulation — synthetic_potential_control
text: StateTransitionData PotentialGroundTruth metadata()$potential_control carrying generating parameters. single experiment "coords" one feature row: simulated x values. colData carries x_coord well ("left"/"right").

title: Generate a synthetic potential control via Langevin simulation — synthetic_potential_control
text: ground truth potential: Wells x = ±1 (minima U) Barrier x = 0 (maximum U wells) Barrier height = U(0) - U(1) = 1

what: Validate a StateTransitionData object at a stage boundary — validate_boundary
title: Validate a StateTransitionData object at a stage boundary — validate_boundary
text: Runs every stage entry. Three outcomes: Proceed — schema matches, invariants hold. Auto-migrate — registered migration path exists. Typed failure — returns StageResult status "failure" rather throwing.

title: Validate a StateTransitionData object at a stage boundary — validate_boundary
Validate a StateTransitionData object at a stage boundary — validate_boundary
text: data StateTransitionData (anything, catch type errors early) required_schema character required schema version stage character stage name used error messages

title: Validate a StateTransitionData object at a stage boundary — validate_boundary
text: StateTransitionData (possibly migrated) success; StageResult status = "failure" failure
